title: Systems and methods for updating an electronic map
abstract: Systems and methods for updating an electronic map of a facility are disclosed. The electronic map includes a set of map nodes. Each map node has a stored image data associated with a position within the facility. The method includes collecting image data at a current position of a self-driving material-transport vehicle; searching the electronic map for at least one of a map node associated with the current position and one or more neighboring map nodes within a neighbor threshold to the current position; comparing the collected image data with the stored image data of the at least one of the map node and the one or more neighboring map nodes to determine a dissimilarity level. The electronic map may be updated based at least on the collected image data and the dissimilarity level. The image data represents one or more features observable from the current position.
claims:
Claims We claim: 1. A method of updating an electronic map of a facility, the method comprising: collecting image data at a current position of a self-driving material-transport vehicle, the image data representing one or more features observable from the current position; searching the electronic map for at least one of (i) a map node associated with the current position and (ii) one or more neighboring map nodes within a neighbor threshold to the current position, the electronic map comprising a set of map nodes and each map node comprises a stored image data associated with a position within the facility; comparing the collected image data with the stored image data of the at least one of the map node and the one or more neighboring map nodes to determine a dissimilarity level; and in response to determining the dissimilarity level exceeds a dissimilarity threshold, updating the electronic map based at least on the collected image data. 2. The method of claim 1 , wherein comparing the collected image data with the stored image data to determine the dissimilarity level comprises: comparing the collected image data with the stored image data of the map node to determine the dissimilarity level. 3. The method of claim 1 , wherein searching the electronic map comprises: in response to determining the electronic map does not include the map node associated with the current position, searching the electronic map for the one or more neighboring map nodes within the neighbor threshold to the current position. 4. The method of claim 3, wherein comparing the collected image data with the stored image data comprises: comparing the collected image data with the stored image data of the one or more neighboring map nodes to determine the dissimilarity level. 5. The method of any one of claims 1 to 4, wherein comparing the collected image data with the stored image data comprises: determining a number of mismatched points between the collected image data and the stored image data; and determining the dissimilarity level based on the number of mismatched points and a total number of points. 6. The method of claim 5, wherein the mismatched points comprises one or more points associated with one of a projection error and a reprojection error between the collected image data and the stored image data. 7. The method of claim 6, wherein the reprojection error comprises a mismatch between the collected image data and the stored image data exceeding an offset threshold. 8. The method of any one of claims 1 to 7, wherein the electronic map comprises the map node associated with the current position, and updating the electronic map based at least on the collected image data comprises: replacing the stored image data of the map node with the collected image data. 9. The method of any one of claims 1 to 7, wherein updating the electronic map based at least on the collected image data comprises: in response to determining the electronic map does not include the map node associated with the current position, adding a new map node to the electronic map for the current position and storing at the new map node the collected image data. 10. The method of claim 9, wherein adding the new map node comprises: connecting the new map node to at least two other map nodes of the electronic map. 1 1. The method of any one of claims 9 and 10 comprises: 

 determining the electronic map includes one or more excessive neighboring map nodes relative to the new map node, the one or more excessive neighboring map nodes being a map node associated with a position that is less than an excessive neighbor distance from the current position of the new map node; and in response to determining the electronic map includes the one or more excessive neighboring map nodes, removing the one or more excessive neighboring map nodes from the electronic map. 12. The method of claim 1 1 , wherein the excessive neighbor distance comprises a minimum distance between two map nodes of the electronic map. 13. The method of any one of claims 1 to 12, wherein updating the electronic map based at least on the collected image data comprises, for each neighboring map node of the one or more neighboring map nodes, comparing the collected image data with the stored image data to determine the dissimilarity level; and in response to determining the dissimilarity level exceeds a node removal threshold, removing that neighboring map node from the electronic map. 14. The method of claim 13, wherein the node removal threshold is greater than the dissimilarity threshold. 15. The method of claim 14, wherein the node removal threshold is approximately 50% to 70% dissimilarity. 16. The method of any one of claims 1 to 15, wherein the neighbor threshold comprises a maximum distance between a position of two map nodes. 17. The method of claim 16, wherein the neighbor threshold comprises a maximum number of map nodes. 18. The method of any one of claims 1 to 17, wherein the dissimilarity threshold comprises a minimum mismatch between the collected image data and the stored image data to indicate the electronic map is outdated, 19. The method of claim 18, wherein the dissimilarity threshold is approximately 30% to 40% dissimilarity. 20. The method of any one of claims 1 to 19 comprises determining the dissimilarity threshold based on at least one of a facility type of the facility and one or more prior updates to the electronic map. 21. The method of any one of claims 1 to 20 wherein collecting the image data at the current position of the self-driving material-transport vehicle comprises: operating the self-driving material-transport vehicle to collect the image data while the self-driving material-transport vehicle is conducting a mission. 22. The method of any one of claims 1 to 21 wherein the position of each map node comprises a coordinate and a heading within a coordinate system assigned to the electronic map. 23. A system for updating an electronic map of a facility, the system comprising: one or more self-driving material-transport vehicles operable to navigate the facility, a self-driving material-transport vehicle of the one or more self-driving material-transport vehicles comprising: a memory to store the electronic map, the electronic map comprising a set of map nodes and each map node comprises a stored image data associated with a position within the facility; one or more sensors to collect image data at a current position of the at least one self-driving material-transport vehicle, the image data representing one or more features observable from the current position; and a processor operable to: search the electronic map for at least one of (i) a map node associated with the current position and (ii) one or more neighboring 

 map nodes within a neighbor threshold to the current position, the neighbor threshold being stored in the memory; compare the collected image data with the stored image data of the at least one of the map node and the one or more neighboring map nodes to determine a dissimilarity level; and in response to determining the dissimilarity level exceeds a dissimilarity threshold stored in the memory, update the electronic map based at least on the collected image data. 24. The system of claim 23 further comprises a fleet management system in communication with the one or more self-driving material-transport vehicles, and wherein the processor is operable to transmit at least an updated map portion of an updated electronic map to the fleet management system and the fleet management system operates to distribute the updated map portion to at least one other self-driving material-transport vehicle of the one or more self-driving material- transport vehicles. 25. The system of any one of claims 23 and 24, wherein the processor operates to compare the collected image data with the stored image data of the map node to determine the dissimilarity level. 26. The system of any one of claims 23 and 24, wherein the processor operates to, in response to determining the electronic map does not include the map node associated with the current position, search the electronic map for the one or more neighboring map nodes within the neighbor threshold to the current position. 27. The system of claim 26, wherein the processor operates to compare the collected image data with the stored image data of the one or more neighboring map nodes to determine the dissimilarity level. 28. The system of any one of claims 23 to 27, wherein the processor operates to: determine a number of mismatched points between the collected image data and the stored image data; and 

 determine the dissimilarity level based on the number of mismatched points and a total number of points. 29. The system of claim 28, wherein the mismatched points comprises one or more points associated with one of a projection error and a reprojection error between the collected image data and the stored image data. 30. The system of claim 29, wherein the reprojection error comprises a mismatch between the collected image data and the stored image data exceeding an offset threshold. 31. The system of any one of claims 23 to 30, wherein the electronic map comprises the map node associated with the current position, and the processor operates to replace the stored image data of the map node with the collected image data. 32. The system of any one of claims 23 to 30, wherein the processor operates to, in response to determining the electronic map does not include the map node associated with the current position, add a new map node to the electronic map for the current position and store at the new map node the collected image data. 33. The system of claim 32, wherein the processor operates to connect the new map node to at least two other map nodes of the electronic map. 34. The system of any one of claims 32 and 33, wherein the processor operates to: determine the electronic map includes one or more excessive neighboring map nodes relative to the new map node, the one or more excessive neighboring map nodes being a map node associated with a position that is less than an excessive neighbor distance from the current position of the new map node; and in response to determining the electronic map includes the one or more excessive neighboring map nodes, remove the one or more excessive neighboring map nodes from the electronic map. 35. The system of claim 34, wherein the memory stores the excessive neighbor distance and the excessive neighbor distance comprises a minimum distance between two map nodes of the electronic map. 36. The system of any one of claims 23 to 35, wherein the processor operates to, for each neighboring map node of the one or more neighboring map nodes, compare the collected image data with the stored image data to determine the dissimilarity level; and in response to determining the dissimilarity level exceeds a node removal threshold, remove that neighboring map node from the electronic map. 37. The system of claim 36, wherein the memory stores the node removal threshold and the node removal threshold is greater than the dissimilarity threshold. 38. The system of claim 37, wherein the node removal threshold is approximately 50% to 70% dissimilarity. 39. The system of any one of claims 23 to 38, wherein the neighbor threshold comprises a maximum distance between a position of two map nodes. 40. The system of claim 39, wherein the neighbor threshold comprises a maximum number of map nodes. 41. The system of any one of claims 23 to 40, wherein the dissimilarity threshold comprises a minimum mismatch between the collected image data and the stored image data to indicate the electronic map is outdated. 42. The system of claim 41 , wherein the dissimilarity threshold is approximately 30% to 40% dissimilarity. 43. The system of any one of claims 23 to 42, wherein: the electronic map includes a facility type of the facility and the memory stores data related to one or more prior updates to the electronic map; and 

 the processor operates to determine the dissimilarity threshold based on at least one of the facility type of the facility and one or more prior updates to the electronic map. 44. The system of any one of claims 23 to 43 wherein the self-driving material- transport vehicle operates to collect the image data while conducting a mission. 45. The system of any one of claims 23 to 44 wherein the position of each map node comprises a coordinate and a heading within a coordinate system assigned to the electronic map.
